title: Occhiolism
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''''A Global Prologue'''''}} {{VoidedTheme|mono}}{{User:PortulTrademark/CSSente}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Anonymous+Pro:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400;1,700&family=Nova+Round&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/code-new-roman-2'); .page__main {background-color: #080808;}}
[[File:OcchiolismStars.png]]
{{Collapse|num=0|final=|initial=
'''Occhiolism:''' Dictionary
occhiolism
/ˈoʊkjoʊˌlɪzəm/

''noun'' (''uncountable'')
The awareness of the small scope of one's own perspective and the way it limits one's ability to fully understand the world.
"I've never been consumed by so much occhiolism, I was so small, so insignificant."
|body=
[[File:FailedMachine.gif|center|frameless|500x500px|link=]] With each strike of the metal ball, Newton's Cradle swings in the opposite direction. But only as many as were first sent down. Every ball comes an equal and opposite response to it. It is only through the balls that this movement occurs, both fluid and seamless the middle ones stay still while the ends moved with energy and dynamism. All Cradles work like this, yet the video denies that basic truth. As the cradle swings, the other balls respond strongly too. Despite only one coming down, 5 swing in protest. Moving with forces of unknown cause. This simple changes breaks the reality of the cradle, far beyond its pixelated look. And yet. It couldn't be more correct. The broad stokes of reality painted perfectly on a failed machine. The perfect consistency the universe tries to show is only as real as our knowledge lacks. An inverse connection. The more we learn about reality, the more unreal it has become. Does that make us the Failed Machine? {{Collapse|num=2|final= |initial= [[File:Stars.png|center|thumb|200px|link=]] |body= [[File:Stars.png|center|thumb|200px|link=]]
Reality thrives upon our own mortality. With the finite existence so insignificant to the universe as a whole we try not to look beyond the barriers that keep us on Earth. We create abstractions and interpretations of the cosmos, beautiful and terrifying, to come to grips with the silence of all that surrounds. The rock we live on, feeling alone. Gravity and Magnetism affix us to the sphere, staying still as Terra and all the others in its celestial neighborhood careen through the Milky Way at 67,000 miles per hour. A speed we can neither fathom nor experience. Yet this abstraction of reality doesn't matter much now, as man has begun again to slip through its cracks. Perhaps a flaw of space itself. The faster it goes, the further it crumbles. Many have learned the hard way what happens when space fails to protect us. {{Quote|1=If you're not careful and noclip out of reality in the wrong areas, you'll end up in the Backrooms, where it's nothing but the stink of old moist carpet, the madness of mono-yellow, the endless background noise of fluorescent lights at maximum hum-buzz, and approximately six hundred million square miles of randomly segmented empty rooms to be trapped in. God save you if you hear something wandering around nearby, because it sure as hell has heard you.|2=An identity forever unknown}} Both natural and artificial, the Backrooms in a space of infinite mazes and locations. Some which we know dearly, and others that are so profoundly other that the mind crumbles trying to deduce where it is. It is the tendrils of our reality, fickle and foreign. Yet among the pseudo-reality, the cosmos still welcome us with open arms. Were are guests on the stage of spacial reality, perhaps the lucky ones get to put on their own show and be forever memorialized amongst the stars. For a time, the Backrooms was only a failure, an experiments from the Acolytes to create freely, as the baseline reality was already set firm. So it became a home for the bodies of flame. The eye bags of solar phenomenon are seen among every step through the unreality, a dying flicker as the cosmological above take their final resting place above the Backrooms. A serene exhibition of something that was truly of the cosmological. Left shielded away from many mans eyes back in objective space, it reveals again in recognition and beauty as man is left unable to burn it away by our own personal stars. A flashlight pales in comparison to the inherent majesty of what lies above us. It comes crashing down. The outreach of cosmological reality has tried to prove its denizens their futility. The solar storms which comes crashing down are the abnormality, a slice of the sun trying to scare mankind to submission again. Its violence has only grown stronger, but so has mans resistance. But no solar storms have been recorded in the Backrooms. The temperament of the universe is a pleased one when they look at a population not drowning itself in light. Within this reality, even the lights man has fashioned do not come close to what exists in the open skies. While night may be wrought with monsters and violence, space provides beauty and serenity as its lowly twinkles often provide peace for those afraid of their endangered mortality. {{Collapse|num=3|final= |initial= But we see ourselves above it all. |body= But we see ourselves above it all. Humanity first wishes for comfort above all things. Food, water, and shelter fit the trifecta of survival. But there in lies a 4th need. Light. The light of a fire as you cook your food, or boil another teapot of water. The lights that dampen a darkened dome turn a place of uncertainty into assurance. The chair that was a creepy figure only covered in close. A man with a hat replaces the standing coat rack. Our sense of reality worsens when we exist in the nighttime. So it is for our own sanity that we see. Lest the mind is driven mad. Humanity made their suns, placing them within their homes and on common streets. They fill their mind with safety, replacing their mind of wonder. Soon their lights obfuscate the ones made by the universe. And there, they start to die again. It was set in stone an endless time ago, so separated from current reality that the attempt to date it has driven the sane insane. For all we can speak of it, through vague insinuations of gospels and manic scribbles, the event was given a name. {{Fullscreen|number=1|padding=4rem |show=
Revisit the Aperature
|hide=
Return to the Macabre
|content={{Blurbackground|file=Spacepixels.jpg}}
Gazing through their exosphere was a sight beyond their understanding. The hand of the janissary touched down on this altered reality. With each touched birthed another path and all its possibilities. It traced out not just stony roads but the routes of mankind, endless in options and direction. And when it wished for plants, it struck the ground with cascading lighting, hammering on the soiled surface till it grew green with virtue and purity. It placed creatures of every size and shape, with bodies of feather, fur, and scales to populate every crevice it manifested. And the sun was set upon it, with a tilt to birth seasons and change with every rotation. A friend of Earth came quickly, shifting the sees and skies to echo the sun's majesty. The three Acolytes looked upon the realm, now doused in flora and atmosphere. They learned their first word: beautiful. And the sights were beautiful.
Atoms and forms came crashing together. In moments, as they speak about the land, it lays itself down, leaving the acolytes a place to walk on. Now the plantae bent to the will of change too, as it tore and formed the way for the trio to travel. Treating them like gods, the floral bodies bowed to the Acolytes. And at the end of their path, they had freedom. Their hands sparked with an otherworldly resonance, another sign of their gift. Creation, it was called. Each command one spoke, it appeared by their desire. And as its eyes could look through the multiple layers of reality, they gazed upon what they loved and made it for their own space. Through endless folds the trio peered deeply into all the facets of the world, now with one graced upon them. While they faced a land of obscurity, the apostles turned that into something with depth, character, and passion. It shifted and contorted to be a place of possible desires and a collection of the things they enjoyed. Graced upon disciples was their second word: good. The world that came from the Acolyte's wishes were good.
The third Acolyte grew jealous however, watching its siblings create the worlds they wanted. And it was responsible for filling it with items and oddities, no monsters and no places. So Acolyte 3 spoke to the stars, giving them life and thought, more than just their hesitant observation. With the strongest army of supporters, it took revenge on the other two siblings, who once denied it creativity. To a court of its own servants, the third made clear its issues. All its creations were cast away and called anomalous, left to rot in a universe they had created. For they were not in line with the other's vision, and had to be expunged. So it turned the tide, painting the other devotees as monsters. [[Acolyte 1|The first apostle]] was blind, its head lobbed off an infinite time ago. It had learned to love the subservient, so it to was made so, unable to speak and only able to react to the misery subjected to it. Its hands torn, rendering it fully unable to touch, to bring things close and show that it wasn't all for the mindless and the enslaved. It wished for control just like the others. [[Acolyte 2|The other]] they thought was too childish to understand beauty and goodness. Like a foolish rat, it was lead along to its cage. Once imprisoned there, it was forced to process the suffering of its siblings actions. Only then did the second become still, wandering a broken plane with what it once created, now unable to understand what they meant. Acolyte 1 lost the ability to understand its senses, and Acolyte 2 lost the ability to understand them at all. [[Acolyte 3|The third assistant]], the judge and executor of its siblings, learned a word; logical. The scarred minds and flesh of the siblings were logical.
Now with the world in the hands of the third, with its siblings left to wander the plane, it became his responsibility. And so the acolyte did, but soon it grew tired, only now discovering the exhaustion of creation. The job it foolishly robbed from their familiars. Through the infinite expanse of time, the task of tying together this false universe became less of the creative visions and means to its end. It became repetitive, dangerous, and malevolent. Acolyte 3 built spaces of misery and torment, as it lost the urge to make each interesting and freeing. The third became lazy, passing the job over to the universe and departed back into the flimsy realms of multireality. When the universe touched it, it blended and broke, stitching together with impossibilities. Phenomenon and entities were put in the excuse the negligence, like the careless builder. When that was not enough it put in things to mitigate the damage, only doing enough. When the third was done, it didn't looks upon its creation as beauty, it saws errs. But when the first man fell in, he learned a new word: real. The buzz of lights that were yet to be discovered and the padding of terra yet to be stepped on was real to him. There was now purpose here. A home.
}} Insignificance became significance, and our unreality became our reality. Humanity took the expanses and tried to [[Empyreanverse|discover something]], [[Abstraction|become something]], [[Ineterna|do something]]. And we did, through their soon centuries of exploration, discovery, and creation. We had become the maestro, trying to create a symphony out of the irregular. It became our home. But it always was known to the ones above as Kuryente. {{Imagebox |file=KuryentePlanet.png|opacity=0.75|height=1000px|outer-style=border:2px solid #080808; border-radius:25px|inner-style= color:#ffe1ff; padding: 20px 10px; font-size:20px; line-spacing:3px; font-family:Nova Round|align=left |text=









}} The third acolyte, now tired of creating ans obscuring the death of those who thought they were close to it promised to give up Kuryente to the cosmos. It was the Backroom's first inhabitants, the trialing tug of the tertiary objects turned a cold sky into a spectacle. Just as the sun and moon did for earth, Kuryente needed it's own visage. A sign to look beyond everything, and with the stars excited to make a new home, Acolyte 3 made that possible for them. But the third made a deal with the cosmos. {{Quote|1=If I come back and I cannot see you shine, I will take the sky as my own again.}} And so it was. Now the realm of Kuryente, the Backrooms, had become their new start. Within the silent howls of a universe unyielding, they spoke to one another. Not by sound, but by the flares and crackles of their solar flesh. Words almost undefined by man, as the cues were too small and too esoteric for the land-dweller's eyes. Yet as its discussion continued, we got better, smarter. Its crackles became another language to master. And one day. We had a lead. {{Collapse|num=4 |initial=
Open Archived log -- March 8th, ████
|final=
Close Archived log -- March 8th, ████
|body=
"When do you think the acolyte will return?" "Which one?" "You know damn well which one I'm talking about." "Okay okay. Honestly, I don't think it will." "Really? They'd just leave us with this beauty and just... disappear?" "Yep. What does it have that would make returning to Kuryente worthwhile for it?" "The issue there is, what wouldn't make it worthwhile?" "Think about it, the acolyte is a reality bender type who likely wants full control over what they created? It only allows us to own this place because it's busy doing other things." "And what other things is that?" "How would I know? No one has seen the third acolyte in millennia." "I follow... but why bring this up?" "Well, look at the wanderers, they've gotten better with creating these grand civilizations and now are easily using the lights already in the level to blot out the night." "We're still the brightest thing in the sky most times, I don't think it's as serious as you think it'll be." "But it'll get more severe. Trust me." "Sure, fine, whatever. But if that's even the case, how would you go about responding to it?" "We don't have some sort of magical abilities to turn off and purge them. We're worse than the [[The Frontrooms|Earthean]] solar mass. None of us can make solar flares. "True. But we'll have to do something." "Once you have any idea what that something will be, let me know."
}} Soon they would know what had to be done. While the strength of the Backroom's many stars is minimal, in a collective, they formed a master plan. The stars learned that they could shut down, and with the limited guise of the elder solars, it created those who could honor it and work for it, [[Entity 13|Solarnoids]]. Further beyond that they found ways to interact creatively with the Backrooms as a whole. Not to show its kindness, but in case it needed to show its wrath. It could [[Phenomenon 199|scorch the sea]] and the land, they learned how to grow still and travel. It became their place to master. They did not fear humanity back then, yet it was a story soon to change. For as man began to dot the land in tiny freckles of light, its silent pollution had the stars begin to fade. The denser levels went first, but it didn't take look to see even the lesser populated locations soon scrub out the sky. The [[Phenomenon 8|tales of heroes and villains etched into space]] became harder to read. And if the stars didn't act, they would no longer call this place home. The third warned them, in fact, leaving panic amongst a sentient universe. {{Quote|1=I will be returning in 3 spans around the celestial maxim. I wish to see what remains of my brothers, and the status of your home. Remember our deal, because I certainly do.|2=Acolyte 3}} }}}}
